title: Plug Puller v5 STL Comparison Configuration
description: Tolerances and required checks for OpenSCAD-vs-golden-fixture mesh comparison. Tight thresholds reflect single-generator determinism (OpenSCAD + Manifold backend).

volume.description: Maximum allowed volume drift vs golden fixture, as a percentage of the reference volume.

surface_area.description: Maximum allowed surface-area drift vs golden fixture, as a percentage.

bounding_box.description: Maximum allowed bounding-box extent drift in millimetres (max per-axis |refExtent - testExtent|).

watertightness.description: Compare test mesh's watertight state to the reference (regression guard). Set `require_test_watertight: true` in a strict profile to forbid any non-watertight output regardless of the reference state. Since the v5.1 fillet rework (slice-based rim flares replaced the minkowski shells) every shipped fixture reference is watertight.

face_count.description: Face count varies legitimately with tessellation noise; informational only.

vertex_count.description: Vertex count varies legitimately with tessellation noise; informational only.

openscad.note: 2026 nightly with the Manifold backend is required for fixture-byte reproducibility. CGAL builds may render geometrically equivalent meshes that differ in vertex order and face count.

notes: Plug-puller has a single generator (OpenSCAD itself); these tolerances guard against toolchain drift, not cross-generator semantic differences.

If a future OpenSCAD release legitimately changes one of these metrics by more than the tolerance, regenerate the golden fixtures (see scripts/README.md) and bump the `last_updated` date in this file.
